name: jq
description: Lightweight command-line JSON processor. Slice, filter, map, and transform structured data.
author: Stephen Dolan

agent_hints.when_to_use: Parse, filter, or transform JSON data from files or piped input

name: yq
description: Lightweight and portable command-line YAML processor. Like jq but for YAML, JSON, XML, CSV, and TOML.
author: Mike Farah

agent_hints.when_to_use: Parse, filter, or transform YAML, XML, CSV, or TOML files

name: csvkit
description: A suite of command-line tools for converting to and working with CSV files.
author: Christopher Groskopf

agent_hints.when_to_use: Convert, analyze, or query CSV files from the command line
example_usage: csvsql --query 'SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM data GROUP BY name' data.csv

name: xsv
description: A fast CSV command line toolkit written in Rust.
author: Andrew Gallant

agent_hints.when_to_use: High-performance CSV processing: search, slice, join, aggregate
example_usage: xsv select name,age data.csv | xsv sort -s age

name: dasel
description: Query and modify data structures from the command line. Supports JSON, YAML, TOML, XML, CSV.
author: Tom Wright

agent_hints.when_to_use: Query or modify structured data files in-place across multiple formats

name: gron
description: Make JSON greppable! Transforms JSON into discrete assignments to make it easier to grep.
author: Tom Hudson

agent_hints.when_to_use: Make JSON greppable by flattening it into assignment statements

name: ripgrep
description: Blazingly fast recursive search tool that respects .gitignore. A modern replacement for grep.
author: Andrew Gallant

agent_hints.when_to_use: Search file contents recursively with regex, much faster than grep

name: fd
description: A simple, fast, and user-friendly alternative to find. Respects .gitignore by default.
author: David Peter

agent_hints.when_to_use: Find files and directories by name or pattern, faster than find

name: fzf
description: A general-purpose command-line fuzzy finder. Interactive filtering for any list.
author: Junegunn Choi

agent_hints.when_to_use: Interactively filter and select from any list of items
example_usage: git branch | fzf | xargs git checkout

name: bat
description: A cat clone with syntax highlighting and Git integration. Like cat but better.
author: David Peter

agent_hints.when_to_use: Display file contents with syntax highlighting

name: eza
description: A modern replacement for ls with colors, icons, Git integration, and tree view.
author: eza-community

agent_hints.when_to_use: List files with details, icons, git status, and tree view

name: tree
description: Display directory structure as an indented tree of files.
author: Steve Baker

agent_hints.when_to_use: Visualize directory structure as an indented tree

name: ncdu
description: NCurses-based disk usage analyzer. Find what's eating your disk space.
author: Benjamin Sago

agent_hints.when_to_use: Analyze disk usage interactively to find large files and directories

name: dust
description: A more intuitive version of du in Rust. Visualizes disk usage with a bar chart.
author: Andy Boot

agent_hints.when_to_use: Quickly see which directories use the most disk space with visual bars

name: httpie
description: Human-friendly HTTP client for the API era. Colorized output, JSON support, sessions.
author: Jakub Roztocil

agent_hints.when_to_use: Make HTTP requests with a human-friendly syntax, good for API testing

name: curl
description: Command-line tool for transferring data with URLs. Supports HTTP, FTP, and many more protocols.
author: Daniel Stenberg

agent_hints.when_to_use: Transfer data to/from URLs, make HTTP requests, download files

name: wget
description: Non-interactive network downloader. Download files from the web, supports recursive downloads.
author: GNU Project

agent_hints.when_to_use: Download files from the web, mirror websites, resume interrupted downloads

name: aria2
description: Lightweight multi-protocol multi-source download utility. Supports HTTP, FTP, BitTorrent.
author: Tatsuhiro Tsujikawa

agent_hints.when_to_use: Download files with multiple connections for faster speed, or download torrents

name: dog
description: A command-line DNS client like dig but with colorful output and DNS-over-HTTPS support.
author: Benjamin Sago

agent_hints.when_to_use: Look up DNS records with colorful output and DoH/DoT support
